channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Microsoft opens door to the past by releasing 86-DOS and PC-DOS 1.00
item.description: Microsoft has released the source code for 86-DOS and PC-DOS 1.00, appealing to retro computing enthusiasts and preserving historical software. This move highlights Microsoft's commitment to open source and nostalgia in tech.

item.title: The Download: storing nuclear waste and orchestrating agents
item.description: The Download newsletter discusses the growing support for nuclear energy and the need for effective nuclear waste management strategies. This reflects a shift in public and political attitudes towards nuclear power.

item.title: 5 ways your Windows updates are about to get a lot less painful
item.description: Microsoft is addressing common frustrations with Windows updates by rolling out improvements in the Windows 11 update experience. These changes are currently available in Insider preview channels.

item.title: EU waves through open source age-check tool to keep kids safe online
item.description: The European Commission has endorsed an age verification app to help online platforms protect children from harmful content. This initiative aims to enhance online safety for minors across EU member states.

item.title: Building AI-ready data: Vanguard’s Virtual Analyst journey
item.description: Vanguard shares insights on developing its Virtual Analyst solution, focusing on AI-ready data principles and AWS services. The initiative has led to significant business improvements.

item.title: Run custom MCP proxies serverless on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: This article explains how to deploy a serverless MCP proxy on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime, emphasizing governance and security alignment. It offers a practical guide for developers.

item.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
item.description: A recent supply-chain attack has targeted security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden, exposing vulnerabilities in their systems. This incident raises concerns about the security of security companies themselves.

item.title: GitHub says sorry and vows to do better as uptime slips and devs complain
item.description: GitHub has publicly apologized for recent uptime issues and developer complaints, promising improvements in service reliability. The statement reflects growing frustrations among users.

item.title: The best VPN services for iPhone in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: This article reviews the best VPN services for iPhone users in 2026, emphasizing privacy and security. Expert testing ensures users can choose reliable options.

item.title: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is likely coming earlier. Here's everything to know so far
item.description: Amazon's Prime Day 2026 is anticipated to occur earlier than usual, with details emerging about the event. This could affect consumer shopping habits and sales strategies.

item.title: GoDaddy customer claims registrar transferred 27-year-old domain without any security checks
item.description: GoDaddy is investigating claims that it transferred a 27-year-old domain to another customer without proper security checks, leading to frustration for the original owner. This incident raises concerns about domain security and customer trust in registrars.

item.title: I've Covered Robots for Years. This One Is Different
item.description: Eka's robotic claw is being hailed as a significant advancement in robotics, capable of performing various tasks like sorting and assembly. This innovation suggests a pivotal moment in the integration of AI into physical tasks.

item.title: Coby Adcock’s Scout AI raises $100M to train its models for war. We visited its bootcamp
item.description: Scout AI has raised $100 million to develop AI agents that assist soldiers in managing fleets of autonomous vehicles. This funding underscores the growing interest in military applications of AI technology.

item.title: AI clause in new SAP API policy has partners worried over lock-in
item.description: SAP's new API policy includes an AI clause that restricts the integration of third-party AI tools, raising concerns among partners about potential lock-in effects. Experts fear this could limit innovation and flexibility for customers.

item.title: The best Apple TV VPNs of 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: This article reviews the best VPNs for Apple TV in 2026, emphasizing ease of setup and streaming capabilities. It aims to help users navigate the complexities of VPN installation on their devices.

item.title: How AI Could Help Combat Antibiotic Resistance
item.description: At WIRED Health, surgeon Ara Darzi discussed how AI could revolutionize the diagnosis and treatment of antibiotic-resistant infections, though he noted that a lack of incentives may hinder innovation's reach to patients.

item.title: Bork in Prague: SUSE's keynote gods demand their tribute
item.description: SUSE's recent keynote in Prague faced technical difficulties, overshadowing its message about European independence in the tech landscape. The event's hiccups serve as a reminder of the challenges in live demonstrations.

item.title: General Motors is adding Gemini to four million cars
item.description: General Motors plans to integrate Google's Gemini AI assistant into four million vehicles, enhancing their infotainment systems through over-the-air updates. This move reflects the growing trend of AI in automotive technology.

item.title: Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age
item.description: OpenAI has proposed a five-part action plan to enhance cybersecurity in the age of AI, focusing on democratizing AI-driven defenses and safeguarding critical systems. This initiative aims to address emerging threats in a rapidly evolving landscape.

item.title: Xiaomi releases MiMo-v2.5 Family weights with strong coding and agent benchmarks
item.description: Xiaomi has introduced its MiMo-v2.5 Family weights, showcasing strong coding and agent benchmarks. The release highlights the company's commitment to advancing technology in the realm of computer science education.

item.title: Hostinger Review 2026 – Honest Assessment After Years of Usage
item.description: Hostinger's 2026 review provides an updated assessment of its speed, uptime, pricing, and support, helping users decide when to choose it over competitors.

item.title: 32 Claude Code Hacks for Smarter Development : Stop Wasting Tokens
item.description: A guide outlines 32 hacks for Claude Code, aimed at enhancing development efficiency and optimizing token usage for developers of all levels.

item.title: Amazon Clears Out Samsung Galaxy Tab A11+ at Its Lowest Price, Now Nearly as Cheap as Galaxy Buds
item.description: Amazon has drastically reduced the price of the Samsung Galaxy Tab A11+, making it nearly as affordable as Galaxy Buds, attracting budget-conscious consumers.

item.title: agent_aid added to PyPI
item.description: The agent_aid library has been added to PyPI, enabling local Windows desktop control for AI agents through a Python library and CLI.

item.title: pysae-ai-tools 0.1.873
item.description: The pysae-ai-tools 0.1.873 release includes shared Python tools designed to enhance Claude Code skills for developers.

item.title: Xen Security Advisory 486 v2 (CVE-2026-23558) - grant table v2 race in status page mapping
item.description: Xen Security Advisory 486 v2 addresses a vulnerability in grant table v2 mapping, providing updates and issue descriptions for users.

item.title: Google launches Ask YouTube, a conversational AI search "experiment" that generates pages with videos and text summaries, for Premium users in the US aged 18+ (Jay Peters/The Verge)
item.description: Google has launched 'Ask YouTube,' a conversational AI search feature that generates pages with videos and text summaries for Premium users in the US.

item.title: mongodb 2.3.0
item.description: MongoDB version 2.3.0 introduces updates including testing for PHP 8.5, reflecting ongoing improvements in the database management system.

item.title: The Executive Collapse Nobody Tracks: Revenue Pressure and the Leadership Health Crisis
item.description: The article discusses the hidden crisis of executive health under revenue pressure, highlighting the impact on leadership effectiveness.

item.title: The Google-Anthropic partnership made trust the strategy behind a $30B run rate
item.description: The partnership between Google and Anthropic is establishing a new standard in enterprise AI, focusing on trust and safety in AI deployments.

item.title: ‘You feel radicalized’: A Meta AI exec watched agents beat her top workers. Now she’s built a nonprofit to help Gen Z find jobs before they disappear
item.description: A Meta AI executive, witnessing the impact of AI on entry-level jobs, has founded a nonprofit aimed at helping Gen Z secure employment before opportunities vanish. The initiative addresses the urgent need for job readiness in a rapidly changing workforce.

item.title: Australia Plans Big Tech Tax to Fund Local News
item.description: Australia is introducing a 2.25% tax on major digital platforms like Google and Meta to fund local news outlets. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's draft legislation aims to ensure tech companies contribute to the news ecosystem.

item.title: Would you buy a ChatGPT-powered iPhone rival? OpenAI is reportedly developing a smartphone chip, which teases the possibility of a full-blown OpenAI mobile
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly developing a smartphone chip designed to integrate AI capabilities directly into mobile devices, potentially reducing reliance on traditional apps. This move hints at the company's ambition to enter the mobile market with a ChatGPT-powered smartphone.

item.title: WATCH: Students take control of bus after driver has medical episode
item.description: Middle school students bravely took control of their school bus after the driver suffered a medical emergency, showcasing quick thinking and teamwork. The incident was captured on video, highlighting their heroic actions.

item.title: Chinese billionaire overhauls AI startup after warning on Manus
item.description: Chinese billionaire Chen Tianqiao is restructuring his AI startup MiroMind by enforcing strict firewalls between his Chinese and US operations. This decision follows regulatory concerns stemming from Meta's Manus acquisition, aimed at safeguarding data and personnel.

item.title: APAC Search Strategy Goes Beyond Google & Baidu via @sejournal, @motokohunt
item.description: The search landscape in the APAC region is evolving beyond dominant players like Google and Baidu, with local engines and AI tools gaining prominence. This fragmentation is setting a new standard for search visibility and success.

item.title: Statement by Russian Defence Ministry
item.description: On April 25, 2026, illegal armed groups, including JNIM and ALF, attempted an armed attack in Mali. The Russian Defence Ministry issued a statement regarding the incident, highlighting the coordinated actions of these groups.

item.title: Save $400 on this 4K-ready MSI gaming PC with an RTX 5070 Ti and 32GB DDR5 — budget-friendly Aegis Z2 rig also ships with eight-core AMD Ryzen CPU and a 2TB SSD, now just $1,849
item.description: The MSI Aegis Z2 gaming PC is now available for $1,849.99 after a $400 discount. It features an eight-core AMD Ryzen CPU, RTX 5070 Ti, 32GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2TB SSD.

item.title: Microsoft VibeVoice: Open-Source Frontier Voice AI
item.description: Microsoft has launched VibeVoice, an open-source voice AI project available on GitHub. This initiative invites developers to contribute to its development, promoting collaboration in voice technology.

item.title: Here's your first look at Google's upcoming 'Proactive Assistance' feature for Gemini - Android Authority
item.description: Google is set to introduce a new 'Proactive Assistance' feature for its Gemini app, enhancing user interaction with upgraded visuals and voices. This feature aims to improve the overall chat experience by providing timely support and suggestions.

item.title: landseer 0.4.1
item.description: Landseer 0.4.1 introduces an SDK for building AI agents with eight primitives, allowing developers to create solutions on their own infrastructure.

item.title: landseer added to PyPI
item.description: Landseer has been added to PyPI, making its SDK for building AI agents more accessible to developers looking to implement AI on their infrastructure.

item.title: The environmental impact of LLMs vs. SLMs
item.description: The environmental impact of large and small language models varies, highlighting the importance of hybrid deployments and sustainable practices to optimize performance and energy use.

item.title: Nation’s Leading Trucking Litigation Law Firms Adopt Anytime AI to Streamline Complex Litigation and Maximize Results
item.description: Leading trucking law firms are using Anytime AI to automate litigation processes, improving efficiency and client outcomes in complex legal cases.

item.title: Who owns the code Claude Code wrote?
item.description: The article discusses the complexities of copyright ownership for code generated by AI, providing insights for developers and builders in the AI space.

item.title: YouTube is testing a chat-style search that cuts the scrolling
item.description: YouTube is testing a chat-style search feature for Premium users, aiming to provide more intuitive results and summaries, though accuracy may vary.

item.title: Lyric Video Studio 1.5.32
item.description: Lyric Video Studio 1.5.32 allows users to create professional lyric videos easily, targeting musicians and content creators looking for visual enhancements.

item.title: graphton 0.2.1
item.description: Graphton 0.2.1 is a framework for creating declarative agents within LangGraph, facilitating development in AI agent creation.

item.title: comfy-env 0.3.29
item.description: Comfy-env 0.3.29 enhances environment management for ComfyUI custom nodes, focusing on CUDA wheel resolution and process isolation.

item.title: Critical Unpatched Flaw Leaves Hugging Face LeRobot Open to Unauthenticated RCE
item.description: A critical unpatched flaw in Hugging Face's LeRobot could allow unauthenticated remote code execution, posing significant security risks.
